title: undefined: FooBar in Go build
content: Check imports and ensure the symbol is exported (capitalized); run go mod tidy to sync dependencies.

title: Edit tool old_string not found
content: Always Read the file first to get current content before using Edit; the file may have changed since you last saw it.

title: Permission denied on file operation
content: Check file permissions with ls -la; you may need to adjust ownership or mode bits before writing.

title: Go test cache returning stale results
content: Use go test -count=1 to bypass the test cache and force re-execution of all tests.

title: Build fails after editing Go source
content: Check for syntax errors, missing imports, and unused variables; run go vet for static analysis hints.

title: Git merge conflicts after pull
content: Resolve conflicts manually by editing the conflicted files; never force-push to shared branches.

title: Type mismatch in function call
content: Verify function signatures and argument types; use type assertions or conversions where the compiler requires them.

title: Nil pointer dereference at runtime
content: Always check error returns before using the returned value; a nil check prevents panics from failed operations.

title: Module not found in Go build
content: Verify the module path in go.mod matches the import; run go mod tidy to add missing or remove unused modules.

title: Context deadline exceeded
content: Increase the timeout for long-running operations or optimize the slow path; check for network issues or blocking calls.

title: Read before Write pattern
content: Always read a file before editing it to ensure you have the current content and avoid stale old_string matches.

title: Plan Mode for multi-file changes
content: Use Plan Mode when changes span 3 or more files to think through the approach before making edits.

title: Test-first approach for bugfixes
content: Write a failing test that reproduces the bug first, then fix the code until the test passes.

title: Incremental changes with verification
content: Make small, focused changes and run tests after each one rather than batching many changes together.

title: Maintain CLAUDE.md conventions
content: Keep a CLAUDE.md file with project conventions, build commands, and architecture notes for consistent AI assistance.

title: Subagents for parallel research
content: Use the Task tool to spawn subagents for independent research tasks that do not require file modifications.

title: Commit before risky changes
content: Commit the current working state before attempting risky refactors or large changes so you can revert if needed.

title: Error handling before happy path
content: Handle error cases and edge conditions first with early returns to keep the happy path at minimal indentation.

title: When to use Plan Mode
content: Enter Plan Mode when the task involves 3 or more files, unclear requirements, or needs architectural discussion.

title: When to run tests
content: Run tests after every meaningful code change; do not batch multiple changes before verifying correctness.

title: When to split sessions
content: Start a new session before the context window fills up, typically around 50 turns or when coherence drops.

title: Grep vs Read tool choice
content: Use Grep for searching across files by pattern; use Read when you know the exact file path to examine.

title: Edit vs Write tool choice
content: Prefer Edit for modifying existing files as it sends only the diff; use Write only for creating new files.

title: When to use subagents
content: Spawn subagents for independent research or exploration tasks; avoid them for changes that need sequential coordination.

title: Git workflow best practices
content: Commit early and often with meaningful messages; use feature branches for larger changes to keep main stable.
